description:
Integration phase: wire P2P transport into launcher telemetry, launcher session handling, and browser terminal view. P2PTransport implements the same sendEvent/onRoomEvent interface as the Matrix client, so it's passed to TerminalSocket's constructor directly (no changes to terminal-socket.js).

Depends on Phase 3 (P2PTransport adapter).

See docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Tasks 9-11.
title: Phase 4: P2P Integration

description:
Plan Task 9: Extend launcher telemetry with P2P discovery

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/launcher/src/runtime.js

**Requirements:**
- Add p2p field to org.mxdx.host_telemetry state event: { enabled: config.p2pEnabled }
- Internal IPs only included when p2pAdvertiseIps is explicitly true (default: false)
- Add #getInternalIps() helper using os.networkInterfaces() — only IPv4, non-internal
- Verify: default config has p2p.enabled but NOT p2p.internal_ips
- Verify: --p2p-advertise-ips true includes p2p.internal_ips

**Security:** Internal IPs NOT included by default. State events persist indefinitely and are visible to all room members, including future joins. p2p_advertise_ips should only be used with trusted, non-public homeservers. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 9
title: Plan Task 9: Extend launcher telemetry with P2P discovery

description:
Plan Task 10: Wire P2P into launcher session handling

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/launcher/src/runtime.js

**Requirements:**
- No changes to terminal-socket.js — TerminalSocket already accepts a client at construction
- P2PTransport implements same interface (sendEvent/onRoomEvent), passed to TerminalSocket constructor
- Add #setupP2P method that:
  1. Fetches TURN credentials via fetchTurnCredentials (validates URL with new URL())
  2. Creates P2PSignaling with m.call.* events
  3. Creates NodeWebRTCChannel with TURN ICE servers
  4. Creates P2PTransport via factory with encryptFn/decryptFn wired to WASM Megolm, signFn/verifySignatureFn wired to Ed25519
  5. Passes P2PTransport to TerminalSocket constructor
  6. Sends m.call.invite, waits for m.call.answer, exchanges ICE candidates
  7. On data channel open: transport.setDataChannel(channel) triggers peer verification
  8. On verification success: switches batch window
  9. On failure: log warning, terminal continues on Matrix
- When config.p2pEnabled is false, use raw Matrix client as before

**Security:** E2EE preserved — encryptFn/decryptFn use WASM Megolm. Peer verification via Ed25519 challenge-response. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 10
title: Plan Task 10: Wire P2P into launcher session handling

description:
Plan Task 11: Wire P2P into browser terminal-view

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/web-console/src/terminal-view.js

**Requirements:**
- No changes to browser terminal-socket.js — same constructor injection pattern
- Add setupBrowserP2P function using BrowserWebRTCChannel:
  1. Fetch TURN credentials (validates URL with new URL())
  2. Create P2PTransport via factory with encryptFn/decryptFn wired to WASM Megolm
  3. Pass P2PTransport to TerminalSocket constructor
  4. Wire onIceCandidateError for TURN-specific errors (486/508/701)
  5. Send invite, wait for answer, exchange candidates
  6. On data channel open: transport.setDataChannel(channel) triggers peer verification
  7. On verification success: switch batch window, update UI to P2P
  8. On status change: update UI indicator
  9. On idle hangup: show Matrix status, reconnect on activity (exponential backoff)
- Read P2P settings from localStorage
- Clamp values: batchMs 1-1000, idleTimeout 30-3600
- Add updateStatus function mapping transport status to UI text and CSS class

**Security:** E2EE preserved. localStorage values validated and clamped to prevent malicious manipulation. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 11
title: Plan Task 11: Wire P2P into browser terminal-view

description:
WebRTC channel wrappers for Node.js and Browser. Provides platform-specific WebRTC data channel abstractions with a common interface (createOffer, acceptOffer, acceptAnswer, addIceCandidate, onMessage, send, etc.).

Depends on Phase 1 (node-datachannel dependency).

See docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Tasks 5-6.
title: Phase 2: P2P WebRTC Wrappers

description:
Plan Task 5: WebRTC channel wrapper — Node.js

**Files:**
- Create: packages/core/webrtc-channel-node.js
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/webrtc-channel-node.test.js

**Requirements:**
- Thin wrapper around node-datachannel PeerConnection
- Interface: createOffer, acceptOffer, acceptAnswer, addIceCandidate, onIceCandidate, onMessage, onClose, onStateChange, send, waitForDataChannel, isOpen, close
- onStateChange callback fires with ICE connection state string
- node-datachannel does NOT expose icecandidateerror — TURN failures detected via state becoming 'failed'
- TDD: write failing test first, test peer-to-peer data exchange with loopback
- Test requires stun:stun.l.google.com:19302 reachable

**Dependencies:** Requires node-datachannel from Task 1.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 5
title: Plan Task 5: WebRTC channel wrapper — Node.js

description:
Plan Task 6: WebRTC channel wrapper — Browser

**Files:**
- Create: packages/web-console/src/webrtc-channel.js

**Requirements:**
- Same interface as NodeWebRTCChannel but using native RTCPeerConnection
- Additional: onIceCandidateError(cb) — browser-only, receives { errorCode, errorText, url } for TURN errors
  - 486: Allocation Quota Reached
  - 508: Insufficient Capacity
  - 701: Cannot reach TURN server
- onStateChange(cb) — fires with iceConnectionState string
- Wires: pc.onicecandidate, pc.addEventListener('icecandidateerror'), pc.oniceconnectionstatechange, pc.ondatachannel
- Verify loads in browser dev console

**Security:** TURN error codes exposed for UI feedback only. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 6
title: Plan Task 6: WebRTC channel wrapper — Browser

close_reason: P2P terminal latency reduced from ~3s to ~15ms avg. Direct P2P working, adaptive batching, separated resize polling.
description: Multiple artificial delays in the P2P data path: 200ms batching on both sides, sequential SessionMux polling blocking on resize, TerminalSocket poll delay, and P2PTransport falling through to Matrix when P2P is active. Target: sub-300ms round-trip keystroke latency.
title: Optimize P2P terminal latency from 3s to <300ms

description:
Plan Task 12: UI indicator styles

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/web-console/src/style.css

**Requirements:**
- Add CSS classes for #terminal-status element:
  - .status-matrix (gray #888)
  - .status-connecting (amber #d4a017)
  - .status-p2p (green #22c55e)
  - .status-matrix-lost (amber)

description:
Plan Task 13: P2P Settings in web console

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/web-console/src/settings.js (or settings view)

**Requirements:**
- Add P2P settings controls using safe DOM methods (createElement, textContent, appendChild):
  - Checkbox: mxdx-p2p-enabled (default: true)
  - Number input: mxdx-p2p-batch-ms (default: 10, clamped 1-1000)
  - Number input: mxdx-p2p-idle-timeout-s (default: 300, clamped 30-3600)
- Persist to localStorage via change handlers
- All numeric values validated and clamped on read AND save

**Security:** Input clamping prevents malicious localStorage manipulation from causing excessive sends or signaling churn. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

description:
Plan Task 3: P2P config fields for launcher and client

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/launcher/src/config.js
- Modify: packages/client/src/config.js
- Modify: packages/launcher/bin/mxdx-launcher.js
- Modify: packages/client/bin/mxdx-client.js

**Requirements:**
- Add p2pEnabled (default: true), p2pBatchMs (default: 10), p2pIdleTimeoutS (default: 300), p2pAdvertiseIps (default: false, launcher-only)
- Add to constructor, fromArgs(), save(), and load() methods
- Add CLI flags: --p2p-enabled, --p2p-batch-ms, --p2p-idle-timeout-s, --p2p-advertise-ips (launcher only)
- No iceServers config — TURN credentials come from homeserver /voip/turnServer
- Reconnect backoff (10s → 5 min exponential) is hardcoded, not configurable

**Security:** p2pAdvertiseIps defaults to false. Internal IPs should only be advertised with trusted, non-public homeservers. State events persist indefinitely. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

close_reason: Closed
description:
Plan Task 4: TURN credential fetching

**Files:**
- Create: packages/core/turn-credentials.js
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/turn-credentials.test.js

**Requirements:**
- fetchTurnCredentials(homeserverUrl, accessToken, fetchFn) — fetches from /_matrix/client/v3/voip/turnServer
- turnToIceServers(turnResponse) — converts to RTCPeerConnection iceServers format
- Uses URL() constructor for safe path construction (no string concatenation)
- Validates https: scheme to prevent credential exfiltration via SSRF
- Disables redirect following (redirect: 'error') to prevent credential leakage
- Returns null for non-https URLs, 404 responses, empty URIs
- TDD: write failing tests first

**Security:** URL validation, SSRF prevention, redirect-following disabled. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 4

description:
P2P signaling via standard Matrix call protocol (m.call.invite/answer/candidates/hangup) and the core P2PTransport adapter with E2EE, peer verification, idle timeout, acks, and reconnect with exponential backoff.

Depends on Phase 2 (WebRTC channel wrappers).

See docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Tasks 7-8.
title: Phase 3: P2P Signaling & Transport

description:
Plan Task 7: P2P Signaling — standard Matrix call protocol

**Files:**
- Create: packages/core/p2p-signaling.js
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/p2p-signaling.test.js

**Requirements:**
- SDP/ICE exchange via m.call.invite/answer/candidates/hangup with glare resolution
- Methods: sendInvite, sendAnswer, sendCandidates, sendHangup, sendSelectAnswer
- waitForInvite/Answer/Candidates/Hangup(timeoutSecs) — poll via onRoomEvent
- resolveGlare(remoteUserId) — lower user_id wins
- Static: generateCallId(), generatePartyId() — both return crypto.randomUUID()
- All events include version: '1' per Matrix call spec v1
- TDD: write failing tests first

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 7
title: Plan Task 7: P2P Signaling — Matrix call protocol

description:
Plan Task 8: P2PTransport adapter with E2EE, peer verification, idle timeout

**Files:**
- Create: packages/core/p2p-transport.js
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/p2p-transport.test.js

**Requirements:**
- Static factory: P2PTransport.create({ matrixClient, encryptFn, decryptFn, signFn, verifySignatureFn, localDeviceId, ... })
- sendEvent(roomId, type, content) — encrypts via encryptFn, routes via data channel or falls back to Matrix. NEVER sends unencrypted terminal data over the data channel.
- onRoomEvent(roomId, type, timeoutSecs) — checks P2P inbox (decrypts via decryptFn), falls through to Matrix polling
- setDataChannel(channel) — connects WebRTC channel, initiates peer verification, starts keepalive + idle timer after verification
- Peer verification: challenge-response with Ed25519 device key signatures. 10s timeout. Failure → close channel, fall back to Matrix
- Maximum frame size: 64KB. Drop and log oversized incoming frames before JSON.parse()
- Idle timer: resets on send/receive, fires onHangup('idle_timeout') when expired
- Reconnect with exponential backoff: 10s → 20s → 40s → 80s → 160s → 300s (5 min cap). Resets after successful P2P session
- Application-level acks: tracks pending, requeues unacked via Matrix on channel close
- Keepalive: ping every 15s, pong timeout 5s
- In-band frame types: encrypted, ack, ping, pong, peer_verify
- TDD: 8+ tests covering Matrix fallback, E2EE, pre-verification blocking, P2P delivery, unacked requeue, oversized frame drop, idle timeout, backoff

**Security:** E2EE mandatory — terminal data is Megolm-encrypted before placement on data channel. Peer identity verified via challenge-response after channel opens. Frame size limits prevent DoS. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 8
title: Plan Task 8: P2PTransport adapter with E2EE, peer verification, idle timeout

title: Phase 6: P2P E2E Testing

description:
Plan Task 14: P2P E2E test with loopback WebRTC

**Files:**
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/p2p-e2e.test.js

**Requirements:**
- createMockPair() helper: two mock Matrix clients relaying events via in-memory queues
- Test flow:
  1. Both sides create P2PSignaling and NodeWebRTCChannel
  2. Side A sends m.call.invite, Side B receives and sends m.call.answer
  3. ICE candidates exchanged via m.call.candidates
  4. Data channel opens, both sides create P2PTransport via factory with mock encrypt/decrypt/sign/verify
  5. Peer verification completes (challenge-response over data channel)
  6. Terminal data sent A→B via P2P — verify data is encrypted on the wire
  7. Verify received data is correctly decrypted
- Separate tests for: idle timeout fallback, exponential backoff, oversized frame drop
- Requires stun:stun.l.google.com:19302 reachable (may need CI skip)

**Security:** Verify E2EE on the wire — inspect raw data channel messages to confirm encryption. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.

Ref: docs/plans/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md Task 14
title: Plan Task 14: P2P E2E test with loopback WebRTC

description:
Plan Task 15: Full system E2E test with Tuwunel

**Files:**
- Modify: packages/e2e-tests/test/terminal-e2e.test.js

**Requirements:**
- Add P2P test case to existing terminal E2E suite verifying:
  1. Terminal session starts on Matrix (immediate)
  2. m.call.invite event appears in room
  3. m.call.answer event appears
  4. peer_verify frames exchanged (challenge-response)
  5. Data channel opens, peer verification completes (status → 'p2p')
  6. Terminal data flows over P2P — verify Megolm-encrypted on data channel
  7. After idle timeout, m.call.hangup appears with reason: 'idle_timeout'
  8. Terminal continues on Matrix
  9. New data triggers fresh m.call.invite (respecting exponential backoff)
  10. Telemetry state event contains p2p.enabled but NOT p2p.internal_ips (default config)

**Security:** Full system verification of E2EE, peer identity, and telemetry privacy. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.
